description: Split the audio signal into fixed length chunks of 8 samples.

properties.note: UI-only: controls whether the container is displayed vertically or horizontally in the scriptnode graph. No effect on audio processing.

gaps.question: How does wrap::fix_block<8, T> handle incoming buffers that are not a multiple of 8? Does ChunkableProcessData produce a remainder chunk smaller than 8, or is the last chunk zero-padded?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether child nodes always see exactly 8 samples or may see fewer in the last chunk. This affects how nodes with state (filters, delays) behave at chunk boundaries.

gaps.question: How are MIDI events distributed across sub-block chunks? Does ChunkableProcessData split events by timestamp so each chunk only sees events within its sample range?
gaps.context: Important for understanding whether modulation driven by MIDI events (e.g., note-on triggering an envelope) has sub-block accuracy within this container.

gaps.question: When bypassed, the existing doc says children still process but with default chain behaviour. Does FixedBlockNode::setBypassed() re-prepare children with the original block size, or does it simply skip the chunking in process()?
gaps.context: The existing phase3 doc mentions A/B comparison via bypass. Need to confirm the exact mechanism: re-prepare vs passthrough.

gaps.question: If the host block size is smaller than 8 (e.g., blockSize=4), does fix_block<8> pass through without chunking, or does it buffer samples across callbacks?
gaps.context: The infrastructure doc says 'if numSamples < BlockSize, forwards directly'. Need to confirm this means no chunking occurs and child sees the smaller buffer as-is.

gaps.question: When already in frame mode (blockSize==1 from a parent frame container), does fix_block skip its chunking logic entirely? The wrap-templates doc mentions withBlockSizeT preserves frame mode.
gaps.context: Affects nested container behaviour. Users may nest fix_block inside frame containers.

notes: Base variant of the fixN_block group. All fixN variants (8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256) share identical C++ logic via wrap::fix_block<N, T> -- only the template parameter N differs. The existing phase3 doc is the most detailed of the group and contains useful information about modulation update rate implications and the bypass A/B workflow. The cppProperties object is empty, which is expected for containers -- containers are not flagged with IsPolyphonic, IsControlNode, etc. in the base data.
